headline.headline_text: Source-conditional Hold at $247.12 (close 2026-06-11, S4): probability-weighted 12-month expected return +3.0% to base-case PT $256.50 (FY27E adjusted operating EPS $13.50 [S4: consensus ~$13.61] x P/E 19.0x [S4: CEG fwd P/E ~18x FY27E]); scenario-weighted range [-32.0%, +56.8%]. This is a de-rate-already-underway call: CEG is the highest-quality IPP (largest US nuclear fleet ~32.4 GW + post-Calpine ~60 GW), but the stock has already fallen ~40% from its $412 high to its 52-week low (~$247) and the headline +50% Street PT (~$367, S4) is a STALE-TARGET artifact actively being cut (revisions negative 3mo). The risk is now more balanced but still two-sided. CONDITIONAL on (i) the FY2026 Adjusted Operating EPS guide ($11.00-12.00, S3) and the >20% base-EPS-CAGR-through-2029 target holding post-Calpine AND (ii) hyperscaler PPA conversions resuming under the June-2026 PJM/FERC co-location framework. If FY2027 Adjusted Operating EPS guides <$13.0 OR a hyperscaler nuclear PPA is repriced/delayed OR the PJM 2028/29 BRA clears <$250/MW-day, the bear branch dominates (de-rate toward VST's ~14-16x); if multiple new premium nuclear PPAs sign AND the >20% CAGR is reaffirmed with Calpine synergies on track, upgrade toward Buy. Cleaner expression remains the long-VST / short-CEG pair, though the ~7-turn premium has compressed from ~8.

recommendation.rationale_one_paragraph: Source-conditional Hold on CEG at $247.12 (S4: 2026-06-11 close). Constellation is the premium/quality leg of the AI-power IPP cohort: the largest US nuclear fleet (~32.4 GW, 21 reactors), scaled by the January-2026 Calpine acquisition (~$26.6B EV) to ~60 GW (now also the largest US gas fleet + ERCOT/CAISO retail), with an investment-grade balance sheet (S&P BBB+ / Moody's Baa1 / Fitch BBB+) and a >20% base-EPS-CAGR-through-2029 management target. Top-3 anchors: A1 FY2026 Adjusted Operating EPS guidance $11.00-12.00 midpoint ~$11.50 (S3, pro-forma Calpine, reaffirmed Q1'26), A2 nuclear fleet ~32.4 GW / 21 reactors (S1: FY2025 10-K), A3 Calpine acquisition closed ~$26.6B (S2: 8-K 2026-01-07). Base PT $256.50 = FY27E adjusted operating EPS $13.50 x P/E 19.0x. The debate is whether a premium ~21x FY26E / ~18x FY27E multiple is durable on what is partly cyclical-peak earnings: PJM capacity has cleared at the FERC cap ($329-333/MW-day) for two consecutive auctions (definitionally a peak), the 45U nuclear PTC floor is ~$0 today at high power prices and sunsets end-2032, the FERC EL25-49 order restricts behind-the-meter co-location (compressing the hyperscaler-PPA premium the multiple assumes; conversions are 'paused but not cancelled'), and the Calpine gas fleet was bought near a cycle peak (vs NRG/VST gas deals at ~7-7.5x) and dilutes the clean-nuclear narrative. Against that, the stock has ALREADY de-rated ~40% from $412 to its 52-week low, the Q1'26 print beat with guidance reaffirmed, and the scarcity value of 24/7 carbon-free nuclear for hyperscaler demand is real. Probability-weighted 12-month return +3.0%, fat-tailed both sides (strong_bear $168.00 / strong_bull $387.50). Earnings quality is high on the usual failure dimensions (PwC clean opinion; IG; no restatement/going-concern), but GAAP net income is non-informational (FY25 GAAP EPS $7.40 vs Adjusted Operating EPS $9.39 due to derivative mark-to-market) and operating cash flow swung deeply negative in 2022-24 on hedge-collateral posting before recovering to +$4.2B in FY25. Net: the best franchise in the cohort at a still-premium price, with the de-rate already substantially done -> Hold; the actionable edge is the long-VST/short-CEG relative-value pair (premium compressing) and a re-entry on a sub-$200 print or a new premium PPA, not a directional long at $247.

investment_thesis_pillars.title: Scarcest asset in the AI-power build: the largest US nuclear fleet
investment_thesis_pillars.key_statistic: ~32.4 GW nuclear, 21 reactors at 15 sites - the largest US fleet; ~60 GW total post-Calpine (S1/S2)
investment_thesis_pillars.narrative: Nuclear is the only proven 24/7 carbon-free baseload, and hyperscalers are signing 20-year PPAs at premium prices for exactly this. CEG has the most of it: the Microsoft/Crane (Three Mile Island Unit 1) ~835 MW restart (20-yr, online ~2027-28) and the Meta/Clinton ~1.1 GW 20-yr PPA convert merchant nuclear into contracted premium annuities, and a ~5 GW pipeline sits in PJM/ERCOT queues. Each new PPA structurally re-rates a slice of the fleet. This scarcity-plus-pipeline is the core of CEG's premium multiple.
investment_thesis_pillars.timeline: Crane online 2027-28; new PPA conversions gated on the June-2026 PJM/FERC framework

investment_thesis_pillars.title: Calpine scale + >20% base-EPS-CAGR engine
investment_thesis_pillars.key_statistic: Calpine (~$26.6B EV, closed Jan-2026) -> ~60 GW; FY2026 Adj Op EPS guide $11.00-12.00; >20% base EPS CAGR 2026-2029 (S2/S3)
investment_thesis_pillars.narrative: The Calpine acquisition makes CEG the largest US power producer, adding ~26 GW modern gas + The Geysers geothermal + a large ERCOT/CAISO retail book (the VST-style integrated hedge). Management guides ~$2/share Calpine accretion and a >20% base Adjusted-Operating-EPS CAGR through 2029, funding a $5B buyback (shares to shrink again after the deal step-up) and ~10%/yr dividend growth on an IG balance sheet. If delivered, the per-share compounding is the bull's engine.
investment_thesis_pillars.timeline: Calpine synergies + LS Power 4.4 GW divestiture (~$5B) through 2026-27

investment_thesis_pillars.title: Investment-grade balance sheet + 45U downside floor
investment_thesis_pillars.key_statistic: S&P BBB+ / Moody's Baa1 / Fitch BBB+; 45U nuclear PTC floor ~$43.75/MWh; ~$5.3B standalone net debt pre-Calpine (S2/S5)
investment_thesis_pillars.narrative: CEG carries the cleanest balance sheet in the cohort (low standalone leverage; IG affirmed post-Calpine with a $3.4B deleveraging plan) and the 45U production tax credit provides a hard price floor on the nuclear fleet (~$43.75/MWh). That combination caps downside relative to higher-levered, unfloored merchant peers - the reason CEG trades at a premium and the reason a full multiple collapse is a tail, not a base case.
investment_thesis_pillars.timeline: 45U floor binds through end-2032 sunset; deleveraging 2026-27

investment_thesis_pillars.title: The bear: a premium multiple on cyclical-peak earnings
investment_thesis_pillars.key_statistic: ~21x FY26E / ~18x FY27E P/E (vs VST ~14x); PJM capacity cleared AT THE CAP $329-333/MW-day; 45U ~$0 today; FERC EL25-49 restricts BTM (S2/S4)
investment_thesis_pillars.narrative: CEG's premium rests on earnings that are partly cyclical-peak: PJM capacity is cleared at the FERC cap (only down from here, floor $179.55), the 45U floor is ~$0 at today's high power prices and disappears in 2032, the FERC EL25-49 order restricts the behind-the-meter co-location economics the premium assumed (hyperscaler conversions are 'paused'), and the Calpine gas fleet was bought near a cycle top and dilutes the clean-nuclear narrative. The whole IPP cohort re-rated up together on the AI theme; the highest-multiple name has the most to lose if it de-rates. Bear lands $150-175 on a multiple-to-mid-teens + estimate-normalization double de-rate.
investment_thesis_pillars.timeline: Bear confirms on a sub-$13 FY2027 guide or a sub-$250 PJM 2028/29 BRA print

company_qualitative.description: Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG) is the largest US producer of carbon-free electricity and, following the January-2026 acquisition of Calpine, the largest US power producer overall (~60 GW). Its core is the largest US nuclear fleet (~32.4 GW, 21 reactors at 15 sites), complemented by hydro, wind, solar, and - post-Calpine - ~26 GW of modern natural gas, The Geysers geothermal complex, and a large competitive retail/commercial business serving ~2.5M customers across PJM, NYISO, ERCOT and CAISO. CEG reports five geographic generation segments (Mid-Atlantic, Midwest, New York, ERCOT, Other Power Regions) plus the commercial (retail + wholesale load-serving) business. FY2025 (standalone, pre-Calpine) revenue was $25,533M (S1: SEC XBRL companyfacts CIK 0001868275) with GAAP net income $2,319M (diluted EPS $7.40) and Adjusted Operating EPS $9.39. GAAP earnings are non-informational quarter to quarter: they are distorted by mark-to-market on the commodity hedge book, and operating cash flow swung deeply negative in 2022-2024 (down to -$5.3B in FY2023) on margin/collateral posting during the gas-price spike before recovering to +$4.2B in FY2025. The 2024-2026 narrative is the AI/data-center power-demand build: CEG signed 20-year nuclear PPAs with Microsoft (Crane/Three Mile Island Unit 1 restart, ~835 MW, online ~2027-28) and Meta (Clinton, ~1.1 GW), and is pursuing a pipeline of additional hyperscaler deals - now gated on the FERC EL25-49 co-location framework due June 2026, which blessed front-of-meter but restricted behind-the-meter structures. The $26.6B Calpine acquisition (closed Jan 7, 2026) adds dispatchable gas + retail and a >20% base-EPS-CAGR target, but also leverage, integration risk, a forced ~4.4 GW PJM divestiture to LS Power (~$5B), and a dilution of the clean-nuclear premium narrative.

products.name: Nuclear generation (~32.4 GW)
products.description: The largest US nuclear fleet (21 reactors, 15 sites) across PJM, NYISO and the Midwest. Carbon-free baseload anchoring the Microsoft/Crane and Meta/Clinton 20-yr PPAs; earns the 45U production tax credit (floor).
products.target_customers: Wholesale ISO markets + hyperscaler 20-yr PPAs (Microsoft, Meta)
products.pricing_model: Merchant energy + capacity + 20-yr PPAs + 45U PTC floor
products.competitive_position: Largest US nuclear fleet; the scarcity asset for 24/7 carbon-free hyperscaler demand

products.name: Natural gas + geothermal (Calpine, ~26 GW)
products.description: Modern CCGT + peaker fleet in ERCOT/CAISO/PJM plus The Geysers geothermal, added via the Jan-2026 Calpine acquisition. Dispatchable capacity earning energy + capacity revenue; ~4.4 GW PJM gas being divested to LS Power.
products.competitive_position: Largest US gas fleet post-Calpine; dilutes the clean-nuclear premium

source_citation.ref: CEG Calpine close 8-K 2026-01-07 + LS Power divestiture 2026-03-18

products.name: Commercial / retail (~2.5M customers)
products.description: Competitive retail + wholesale load-serving across PJM/NYISO/ERCOT/CAISO; one of the largest US competitive suppliers, acting as the integrated hedge for generation.
products.target_customers: C&I and residential customers in deregulated markets
products.pricing_model: Retail margin over hedged supply cost
products.competitive_position: Top-tier competitive supplier; integration smooths merchant volatility (VST-style)

company_qualitative.customers_gtm: CEG sells energy + capacity into ISO/RTO markets (PJM/NYISO/ERCOT/CAISO) plus long-term IG-counterparty nuclear PPAs (Microsoft, Meta). The commercial business serves ~2.5M retail/wholesale customers, internally hedging generation. Counterparty quality on the marquee PPAs is top-decile (hyperscalers). The hyperscaler-PPA pipeline (~5 GW in queue) is the swing growth variable, currently gated on the FERC EL25-49 co-location framework.

industry.overview: US power demand is growing at its fastest rate since 2000 on data-center/AI load. Merchant IPPs (Constellation, Vistra, NRG, Talen) re-rated together through 2024-25 on the AI-electricity theme: PJM capacity cleared at the FERC offer cap for two consecutive auctions (2026/27 $329.17, 2027/28 $333.44/MW-day UCAP) and hyperscalers procured multiple GW of nuclear. The cohort is now mid-to-late cycle: the FERC EL25-49 order (Dec-2025) restricted behind-the-meter co-location (the Talen/Susquehanna BTM structure was rejected; FTM is the survivable path), and the whole group is de-rating in 2026 (CEG -40% off its high) as the market tests how much of the AI premium is durable vs cyclical. CEG is the quality/nuclear-purity leg; Vistra the value/integrated leg.

source.ref: US wholesale power + capacity + retail dollarized; EIA/PJM load-growth forecasts (data-center-driven)

peers.name: Vistra Corp.
peers.key_differentiator: Value/integrated leg: cheaper (~14x P/E / ~10x EV/EBITDA), higher FCF yield (~7.2%), more leverage; pricing in less of the AI thesis

peers.name: NRG Energy
peers.key_differentiator: Retail-heavy + LS Power gas; re-rated to ~18x P/E on its own AI narrative; minimal nuclear

peers.name: Talen Energy
peers.key_differentiator: Nuclear-heavy small-cap (Susquehanna-AWS, restructured to FTM); ~16x P/E; ramping EBITDA

competitive_landscape.positioning_narrative: CEG is the quality/nuclear-purity leg of the AI-power cohort: the largest, scarcest carbon-free baseload fleet, the cleanest balance sheet (IG), and the 45U floor - which justify a premium. But the premium (~7 P/E turns / ~3.7 EV/EBITDA turns over Vistra) is being tested: it rests partly on cyclical-peak PJM capacity (cleared at cap) and hyperscaler-PPA economics now constrained by FERC's BTM restriction, and the Calpine gas addition dilutes the clean-nuclear narrative. The cohort re-rated up together and is now de-rating together; the highest-multiple name has the most to lose. On a 12-month horizon the dominant variable is whether the premium holds as PPA conversions resume (FTM) and the >20% CAGR is delivered, vs a double de-rate toward Vistra's multiple on cyclical normalization. The long-VST/short-CEG pair expresses the premium compression, but the gap has already narrowed from ~8 to ~7 turns.

management.name: Joe Dominguez
management.title: President & CEO
management.bio: President & CEO of Constellation since the 2022 spin from Exelon; architect of the nuclear-to-hyperscaler PPA strategy (Microsoft/Crane, Meta/Clinton) and the Calpine acquisition. Long Exelon/Constellation tenure in policy, generation and competitive markets.

management.name: Dan Eggers
management.title: EVP & CFO
management.bio: CFO responsible for the capital-allocation framework ($5B buyback, ~10%/yr dividend growth), the Calpine financing + deleveraging plan (~$3.4B paydown 2026-27), and the investment-grade balance sheet.

source.ref: Modeled energy-margin allocation: PJM nuclear+gas (CEG does not disclose segment GM; reports geographic segments by capacity; IPP gm-taxonomy adaptation)

entries.name: Consolidated energy gross margin LTM-Q1FY26 analog (~23.75%): revenue less fuel + purchased power + direct operating cost (IPP analog to GM; CEG reports geographic segments + Adjusted Operating Earnings, not a GAAP segment GM)

source.ref: Modeled LTM weighted reconciliation; IPP gm-taxonomy adaptation (analogous to NIM for banks)

entries.name: Segment energy margin: PJM (Mid-Atlantic+Midwest, nuclear-heavy) ~30%; New York ~25%; ERCOT (gas/retail) ~18%; Commercial ~12%

source.ref: Modeled segment energy-margin allocation; reconciles to consolidated within rounding

entries.name: Contracted nuclear (Microsoft/Meta 20-yr PPAs at premium) vs open merchant nuclear/gas margin

source.ref: Modeled: hyperscaler PPA pricing premium to merchant (Microsoft/Crane, Meta/Clinton 8-Ks)

entries.name: FY27E blended generation margin with Calpine gas + uprates in the mix
Marginal open-book margin sensitivity to PJM capacity + power price (the cyclical-peak lever)
entries.value_range: At PJM cap ($329-333/MW-day) + high power: ~24%; at capacity-floor ($179.55) + open book repricing: ~20-21% (FY27 EBITDA swing -$0.8 to -$1.5B; the bear's double-derate driver)

source.ref: PJM 2026/27 + 2027/28 BRA cleared at cap (S2); floor $179.55; CEG out-year book substantially open (hedge ratios not disclosed at this granularity)

methods.narrative: Primary IPP method per D8 (EV/EBITDA). 13.2x FY27E Adjusted EBITDA $8.5B -> EV $112.5B less net debt + NCI $21.15B = $91.4B / 357.1M = ~$256. The 13.2x is a ~3-turn premium to VST's ~10.1x - narrower than the ~3.7-turn current gap, reflecting a modest premium compression as the Calpine gas mix dilutes the clean-nuclear multiple.

10y DCF; WACC 8.17% (CoE 9.13% at beta 1.09 + after-tax Kd 4.35%; 80/20 E/D), g 2.25%. Base ~$215, below spot - disciplines the cyclical-peak earnings (PJM capacity at cap, 45U floor ~$0 today and sunsetting 2032), and reflects that the market price embeds AI-power/PPA-conversion optionality a base DCF cannot underwrite.

Sum-of-parts: nuclear (contracted + scarce) at 11.0x, Calpine gas/geothermal 8.0x, commercial 7.0x, less net debt + NCI. Monotonicity NI<=OP<=GP<=Revenue holds per segment (G3). ~$245/share, between DCF and comps; the gas/retail addition pulls the blended multiple below standalone-nuclear CEG.

Bear floor reference: a double de-rate to VST's ~14-16x on a normalized $12.00 FY27E EPS (PJM capacity reversion + hyperscaler capex cut + Calpine synergy miss) -> $168-192; severe ~$150. Not weighted; downside discipline anchoring strong_bear $168.00 and bear $204.00.

valuation.reconciliation_narrative: Comps $256.5 (primary, D8 IPP EV/EBITDA), SOTP $245, DCF $215 cluster $215-256; the 5-scenario probability-weighted base PT is $256.50 (FY27E Adjusted Operating EPS $13.50 x P/E 19.0x), aligned with the comps method. DCF below spot reflects cyclical-peak earnings (PJM capacity at cap, 45U ~$0 today) plus AI-power optionality a base DCF will not underwrite; bear floor $150-204 (VST-level multiple on a normalized EPS) provides downside discipline and is the 52-week-low region. Methods cross-check rather than average.

risks.title: Premium-multiple de-rate on cyclical-peak earnings
risks.description: CEG trades ~21x FY26E / ~18x FY27E P/E (a ~7-turn premium to VST) on earnings partly inflated by PJM capacity cleared at the FERC cap ($329-333/MW-day, definitionally peak; floor $179.55) and high power prices. The whole IPP cohort re-rated up together and is de-rating together; the highest-multiple name has the most to lose. A double de-rate (multiple to mid-teens + estimate normalization) lands $150-175.
risks.mitigants: Stock already -40% from $412 to 52-wk low; 45U floor + IG balance sheet cap a full collapse; nuclear scarcity is real

risks.title: FERC co-location / behind-the-meter restriction (EL25-49)
risks.description: FERC's Dec-2025 order restricted behind-the-meter large-load co-location (the Talen/Susquehanna BTM structure was rejected) and blessed only front-of-meter, which forces data centers to pay full network/transmission charges - compressing the premium PPA margin CEG's multiple assumed. Management has hyperscaler conversions 'paused but not cancelled' pending the June-2026 framework.
risks.mitigants: CEG's large sites are grid-interconnected (FTM-survivable, less BTM-exposed than a pure island play); conversions delayed not dead

risks.title: Calpine integration / bought-at-peak risk
risks.description: The ~$26.6B Calpine deal (closed Jan-2026) added ~26 GW gas + leverage at a cycle peak (vs NRG/VST gas deals at ~7-7.5x EV/EBITDA), dilutes the clean-nuclear narrative, and forces a ~4.4 GW PJM divestiture to LS Power (~$5B, pending DOJ/FERC). ~50M shares issued; a ~25M-share Calpine seller lockup unlocks 2026-06-30. Synergy/accretion tracking (~$2/share) is the watch item.
risks.mitigants: IG ratings affirmed post-close; $3.4B deleveraging plan 2026-27; retail adds a VST-style hedge

risks.title: Hyperscaler AI-capex cut (the correlated tail)
risks.description: The entire premium correlates to sustained hyperscaler power demand (2026 hyperscaler spend cited ~+75% YoY). A single Microsoft/Meta/Amazon/Google capex guide-down would simultaneously compress the multiple, stall PPA conversions (already paused), and soften forward power curves - one catalyst, three transmission channels.
risks.mitigants: 20-yr signed PPAs (Microsoft/Meta) are contracted; nuclear scarcity persists structurally; low short interest = no forced unwind

risks.title: 45U nuclear PTC: ~$0 today, hard sunset 2032
risks.description: The 45U PTC floor (~$43.75/MWh phase-out) is ~$0 at today's high power prices (a floor, not a current contributor) and sunsets end-2032 - removing the downside backstop exactly where DCF terminal weight is heaviest. FY25 GAAP EPS fell partly on lower PTC top-up.
risks.mitigants: Only binds in the scenario where the equity is already impaired (low prices); transferability preserved through 2032

risks.title: Hedge-collateral / cash-flow volatility
risks.description: Operating cash flow swung deeply negative in 2022-2024 (down to -$5.3B FY23) on margin/collateral posting during the gas-price spike before recovering to +$4.2B FY25. A renewed forward-price spike could demand large collateral again (the VST-analog liquidity tail).
risks.mitigants: IG balance sheet + large credit facilities; FY25 OCF recovery; integrated retail hedge reduces net exposure

risks.title: Stale-target / crowded-long unwind
risks.description: The ~+50% headline Street PT (~$367) is a stale-target artifact being actively cut (Raymond James, Morgan Stanley, Argus lowered in Jun'26); ~76% Buy / 0 Sell is a crowded long with no marginal bull left to convert - an unwind risk as targets re-base to the de-rated price.
risks.mitigants: De-rate already largely played out (-40%); low short interest; IG + buyback + passive absorb sellers

catalysts.title: PJM/FERC co-location framework (EL25-49 compliance)
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: A workable FTM framework that lets paused hyperscaler PPAs convert supports the bull; a restrictive outcome that keeps conversions stalled confirms the bear.

catalysts.title: CEG Q2 FY26 earnings
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: Reaffirmed/raised >20% CAGR + on-track Calpine synergies support Hold->Buy; any guide trim or synergy miss triggers the de-rate. Implied move ~+/-5-7%.

catalysts.title: Calpine seller lockup expiry (~25M shares)
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: Mechanical near-term share overhang; orderly absorption is neutral-positive, heavy selling pressures the stock.

catalysts.title: LS Power 4.4 GW PJM divestiture close
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: Close at ~$5B clears the regulatory condition (positive); a markdown or extended DOJ/FERC review signals overpay-at-peak crystallizing.

catalysts.title: Additional hyperscaler nuclear PPA
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: A new 20-yr premium nuclear PPA (FTM) is the dominant bullish reverse trigger; continued silence keeps the pipeline optionality un-priced.

catalysts.title: PJM 2028/29 Base Residual Auction
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: Clear near the cap supports the open-book; a clear <$250/MW-day (vs ~$333 cap) confirms capacity-peak reversion (bear).

what_would_reverse.numerical_threshold: A new 20-year hyperscaler nuclear PPA signs (FTM) AND the >20% base-EPS CAGR is reaffirmed with FY2027 Adj Op EPS guide >=$13.5
PJM/FERC June-2026 framework enables paused hyperscaler conversions AND PJM 2028/29 BRA clears >$300/MW-day
what_would_reverse.observable_via: FERC EL25-49 compliance + PJM BRA report

what_would_reverse.numerical_threshold: FY2027 Adjusted Operating EPS guide <$13.0 (below the ~12% CAGR off $11.50) OR the >20% CAGR framing is cut/withdrawn
A hyperscaler nuclear PPA (Microsoft/Meta/new) is renegotiated, delayed past 2027, or repriced to FTM economics; OR paused conversions do NOT complete after the June-2026 framework
PJM 2028/29 BRA clears <$250/MW-day UCAP (vs ~$333 cap) - capacity-peak rolls over on the open out-year book
Calpine synergies/accretion tracking <$2/share OR the LS Power 4.4 GW divestiture closes at a markdown / faces extended DOJ-FERC review
Power prices fall enough that 45U PTC re-engages as a floor AND the stock has de-rated below $200 (risk/reward inflects favorably)

tail_risks.name: Hyperscaler AI-capex cut / PPA renegotiation (the dominant correlated narrative tail)
FERC co-location framework (EL25-49) lands restrictive / BTM economics killed
PJM capacity / power-price reversion from cap toward floor (open out-year book)
Fed rate shock +200bp on a premium long-duration multiple
Nuclear scarcity supercycle: multiple new premium PPAs + capacity durability (the bull thesis)
Calpine synergy beat + buyback compounding on the de-rated share price

asc_606_red_flags.flag: Derivative mark-to-market distorts GAAP earnings (interpretation flag, not manipulation)
asc_606_red_flags.evidence: FY25 GAAP EPS $7.40 vs Adjusted Operating EPS $9.39; FY24 GAAP $11.89 vs adjusted $8.67 (GAAP > adjusted on favorable MtM). Underwrite on Adjusted Operating Earnings, not GAAP NI.

asc_606_red_flags.flag: Operating cash flow swung deeply negative 2022-2024 on hedge-collateral posting
asc_606_red_flags.evidence: OCF -$5.3B FY23 / -$2.5B FY24 on margin posting during the gas spike, recovering to +$4.2B FY25. A renewed forward-price spike could demand collateral again (VST-analog liquidity tail).

forensic_flags.vie_exposure: CENG / nuclear joint-venture structures; Calpine project entities + assumed non-recourse debt

antitrust_open_matters.matter: Calpine acquisition + LS Power 4.4 GW divestiture (FERC Section 203 + DOJ); divestiture pending close

sector_regulator_open_matters.matter: Co-location / large-load interconnection framework (Docket EL25-49); FTM blessed, BTM restricted; PJM compliance framework due June 2026
Operating licenses, uprates, and the Crane (TMI Unit 1) restart licensing
Capacity cap/collar; 2026/27 + 2027/28 BRA cleared at cap; 2028/29 BRA upcoming

kelly_math.formula: (E[R]-Rf)/sigma^2 = (0.030-0.0452)/0.1764 = -0.0862; x0.15 conviction = -0.013 (effectively flat -> benchmark weight; Hold)

long_only_large_cap.mandate_constraint: Hold at ~benchmark (S&P 500 CEG weight ~0.18%); premium multiple two-sided; add only on a sub-$200 entry or a new premium hyperscaler PPA

long_only_smid.mandate_constraint: Hold/modest; power mandates may prefer VST for the cheaper integrated exposure or TLN for higher-beta nuclear-pure

long_short_hedge_fund.mandate_constraint: Express the premium compression as a small long-VST / short-CEG market-neutral pair (CEG the funding short), NOT an outright CEG short into a 20-yr-PPA-backed nuclear franchise

sector_specialty.mandate_constraint: Hold near benchmark; underweight vs VST within the IPP sleeve on the premium; overweight only on a sub-$200 print or new PPA

pair_trade.rationale: Long VST (~14x, ~7.2% FCF yield) / short CEG (~21x) on the ~7-turn P/E premium compressing as the Calpine gas mix dilutes CEG's clean-nuclear multiple and the cohort de-rates. BUT the gap has already narrowed from ~8 to ~7 turns (CEG de-rating faster), CEG's IG balance sheet + 45U floor cushion the short, and a new premium nuclear PPA is a squeeze risk; size SMALL and beta-hedge (~0.92), stop if the premium re-expands past ~9 turns on a hyperscaler-PPA headline.

edge_decay.primary_decay_driver: FERC EL25-49 framework (June 2026) + hyperscaler PPA conversions + PJM capacity prints + Calpine synergy tracking

consensus_variance.line_item: CEG forward P/E premium durability (cyclical-peak earnings)
consensus_variance.narrative: The stale ~+50% Street PT (~$367) and 76%-Buy consensus extrapolate the premium ~21x FY26E multiple and the >20% CAGR while implicitly marking PJM-capacity-at-cap and hyperscaler-PPA economics as durable. Our downward variance marks (i) PJM capacity cleared at the FERC cap as a definitional peak (only down; floor $179.55), (ii) the FERC EL25-49 BTM restriction as compressing the PPA premium (conversions paused), and (iii) the Calpine gas mix as diluting the clean-nuclear multiple; this shifts ~2.2pp of probability into the bear tails (a de-rate toward VST's ~14-16x). Two-tailed (nuclear scarcity + the de-rate already done are genuine support), hence Hold not Sell. Sizing = magnitude 18% x probability 60% x sensitivity 20% / 10000 = 2.16.

evidence_refs.ref: PJM 2026/27 ($329.17) + 2027/28 ($333.44) BRA cleared at the FERC cap; floor $179.55 (PJM BRA reports)
FERC EL25-49 order (Dec-2025): BTM large-load co-location restricted; conversions 'paused' (CEG Q1'26)
Street PT median ~$367 stale/being cut (revisions -8% 3mo); CEG fwd P/E ~21x vs VST ~14x

revision_velocity.breadth_note: PT actions net DOWN (Raymond James, Morgan Stanley, Argus cut in Jun'26); avg PT $400->$377 over 3mo. EPS estimates roughly flat (guidance reaffirmed). 76% Buy / 19% Hold / 5% Sell.
revision_velocity.pt_revisions_recent: Avg PT ~$367-377, down from ~$400 3mo ago; still ~+50% above the de-rated spot (a stale-target artifact). Targets re-basing toward the price.
revision_velocity.pre_print_drift: Stock -40% off the $412 high to 52-wk low ~$247; next print ~2026-08-05; implied move ~+/-5-7%.
revision_velocity.peer_comparison: Whole IPP cohort de-rating together; CEG de-rating faster than VST (premium gap 8->7 turns). NRG re-rated up to ~18x on its own narrative.
revision_velocity.verdict: De-rate underway (multiple compression + stale targets being cut), with EPS guidance reaffirmed; our caution is premium durability on cyclical-peak earnings, partly offset by the ~40% drawdown already taken.

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: The AI-power premium deflates: PJM capacity reverts toward the floor, hyperscalers cut capex and PPAs stall, Calpine synergies disappoint, and CEG de-rates to a VST-level ~14x on normalized ~$12 EPS.
scenarios.narrative_long: A hyperscaler capex guide-down fires all three channels at once - multiple compression, stalled PPA conversions, softer power curves. PJM capacity reverts from the cap toward the $179.55 floor on the open out-year book; the 45U floor (~$0 today) only protects a much lower earnings level; Calpine gas margins normalize from peak. The market re-rates CEG to Vistra's ~14x on a normalized $12.00 FY27E EPS.
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 14x P/E = de-rate to Vistra's level - the premium fully erased as the AI/nuclear-scarcity narrative deflates and the gas mix dominates.

eps_bridge.label: PJM capacity reverts from cap toward CONE floor on the open out-year book
eps_bridge.source_tag: S2: PJM BRA cap $333 vs floor $179.55

eps_bridge.label: Calpine synergies disappoint + gas-margin normalization
eps_bridge.source_tag: S3: Calpine gas bought near peak vs NRG/VST ~7-7.5x

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: Soft de-rate: power/capacity normalizes modestly, FERC FTM economics compress PPA margin and delay conversions, and the multiple compresses to ~16x on ~$12.75 EPS - the stock works toward the bear floor.
scenarios.narrative_long: The premium partially erodes as the cohort de-rates and the Calpine gas mix dilutes the multiple. PJM capacity stays above floor but off peak; hyperscaler conversions are delayed (FTM) rather than dead. Multiple settles ~16x - above the trough but below the recent ~21x - on a $12.75 FY27E EPS.
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 16x = partial de-rate; between VST (~14x) and CEG's recent ~21x, pricing cyclicality + the gas-mix dilution.

eps_bridge.label: Power / capacity normalization (partial)
eps_bridge.source_tag: S2: PJM capacity off peak; ERCOT/CAISO spark normalization

eps_bridge.label: FERC FTM economics compress PPA margin + conversion delay

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: The de-rate stabilizes: Calpine delivers ~$2/share accretion, the >20% CAGR holds, PPA conversions resume FTM post-June framework, and CEG holds ~19x (FY27E) - roughly its current multiple - on $13.50 EPS.
scenarios.narrative_long: FY2026 guidance ($11-12) is met and Calpine accretion (~$2/share) plus uprates support the >20% base-EPS CAGR. The June-2026 FERC framework lets paused hyperscaler PPAs convert (FTM, at a modest margin haircut). The premium compresses only modestly as the gas mix dilutes the multiple; the stock holds ~19x FY27E - essentially where it trades now after the ~40% drawdown - on $13.50 EPS.
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 19x FY27E ~ in line with the current ~18x forward after the de-rate; a modest premium to VST justified by nuclear scarcity + IG + 45U floor, net of the Calpine gas dilution.

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: PPA conversions resume and new deals sign, the >20% CAGR is reaffirmed with Calpine on track, and CEG re-rates to ~22x on $14.50 EPS as the nuclear-scarcity premium re-expands.
scenarios.narrative_long: The June-2026 framework enables FTM hyperscaler PPAs; CEG signs incremental premium nuclear deals and delivers the >20% CAGR with Calpine synergies on track. The market re-credits the scarcity premium and re-rates to ~22x on a $14.50 FY27E EPS - back toward where CEG traded before the drawdown.
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 22x = re-rate toward CEG's pre-drawdown multiple; premium re-expands on PPA conversion + delivered CAGR.

eps_bridge.label: >20% base-EPS CAGR delivery (Calpine accretion + uprates)
eps_bridge.source_tag: S3: CEG >20% base EPS CAGR 2026-2029 target

eps_bridge.label: Hyperscaler PPA conversions resume FTM post-June framework

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: Nuclear-scarcity supercycle: multiple new premium PPAs, capacity durability proven, Calpine synergy beat + buyback compounding on the de-rated price; CEG re-rates to ~25x on $15.50 EPS.
scenarios.narrative_long: Structural AI-power demand sustains a multi-year nuclear premium; CEG converts a large share of its ~5 GW pipeline to premium long-dated PPAs, beats Calpine synergies, and compounds EPS via buyback at the de-rated price. Multiple approaches ~25x on a $15.50 FY27E EPS.
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 25x approaches CEG's pre-drawdown peak multiple; tail re-rate predicated on durable premium-PPA conversion + a demand supercycle.

eps_bridge.label: Multiple incremental premium hyperscaler nuclear PPAs (scarcity re-rate)
eps_bridge.source_tag: S4: ~5 GW pipeline in PJM/ERCOT queues

eps_bridge.label: Calpine synergy beat + buyback compounding on the de-rated price

headline.headline_text: CEG at $247.12 carries a probability-weighted 12-month expected return of +3.0% (source-conditional Hold); the call is conditional on the FY2026 Adjusted Operating EPS guide ($11-12, S3) and the >20% base-EPS CAGR holding post-Calpine, and on hyperscaler PPA conversions resuming under the June-2026 FERC framework - scenario-weighted range strong_bear $168.00 (-32.0%) to strong_bull $387.50 (+56.8%). The de-rate is already ~40% done (off the $412 high to a 52-wk low), and the headline +50% Street PT is a stale-target artifact being cut; the premium on cyclical-peak earnings keeps it two-sided.

memo_metadata.net_leverage_note: EV-implied net debt + NCI ~$21.15B (EV $109.4B less mkt cap $88.25B) is used in EV/EBITDA; standalone pre-Calpine net debt was ~$5.3B (total debt $8.9B less cash $3.6B), stepped up by the ~$12.7B assumed Calpine net debt + $4.5B cash deal funding. Out-year hedge ratios are not disclosed at this granularity - the open-book sensitivity is modeled.
